audienceSections.normal: In Luis and Ana’s morning routine, you will use [[grammar:subject + verb + object]]. A simple statement can put a subject, verb, and object in that order. After Luis is clear, También come pan leaves the subject out. Do not repeat the subject pronoun in every Spanish sentence.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter presents [[grammar:subject + verb + object]] in Luis and Ana’s morning routine. Present verbs encode person and number. An overt lexical subject establishes discourse reference, allowing subsequent null subjects while direct objects follow the finite verb.

audienceSections.normal: A simple statement can put a subject, verb, and object in that order. After Luis is clear, También come pan leaves the subject out. Do not repeat the subject pronoun in every Spanish sentence.
audienceSections.expert: Present verbs encode person and number. An overt lexical subject establishes discourse reference, allowing subsequent null subjects while direct objects follow the finite verb.

audienceSections.normal: Read the Spanish punctuation with the sentence: ¿ starts a question and ¡ starts an exclamation. Learn nouns with el or la when an article is shown.
audienceSections.expert: The reading uses broadly intelligible contemporary Spanish with tú as neutral informal singular address. Null subjects and article-bearing citation forms are analyzed only where attested.

breakdown.normal:
Read each exact spanish line, then check its natural English meaning. Watch for [[grammar:subject + verb + object]] where it appears.

- Luis toma café por la mañana. — Luis drinks coffee in the morning.
- También come pan. — He also eats bread.
- Después lee el libro. — Afterward he reads the book.
- Ana bebe agua. — Ana drinks water.
- Luego prepara la mochila. — Then she prepares the backpack.
- Los dos salen de la casa. — The two leave the house.
breakdown.expert:
These exact reading lines provide the distributional evidence for [[grammar:subject + verb + object]]. The grammar section above analyzes the relevant morphology and syntax.

- Luis toma café por la mañana. — Luis drinks coffee in the morning.
- También come pan. — He also eats bread.
